title: populus × canadensis var. regenerata
intro: Populus × canadensis var. regenerata, also known as the hybrid black cottonwood or a specific cultivar of Black Cottonwood (Populus trichocarpa), is a unique and fast-growing tree species. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been used to treat various health issues. The bark of Populus × canadensis var. regenerata contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, offering relief from pain, inflammation, and fever. Herbal preparations such as teas, salves, and infusions can be made from the bark, providing potential health benefits like reducing swelling, improving circulation, and soothing digestive issues.
